title: List Restore Points Before a Drill
hook: A restore drill starts by proving which backups actually exist.
problem: You need a quick, timestamped list of available backup restore points before choosing one to test.
command: cd restore-dr && find backups -maxdepth 2 -type f -name MANIFEST.txt -printf '%TY-%Tm-%Td %TH:%TM %h\n' | sort -r
starting_state: A restore-dr fixture has dated backup directories with MANIFEST.txt files.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads manifest paths and prints their backup directories.
when_to_use: Use at the start of a restore drill or incident to inventory candidate recovery points.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat this as proof the archive contents are valid; it only proves manifests exist.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Dated backup directories sorted newest first.

commands: cd restore-dr && find backups -maxdepth 2 -type f -name MANIFEST.txt -print | sort
cd restore-dr && find backups -maxdepth 2 -type f -name MANIFEST.txt -printf '%TY-%Tm-%Td %TH:%TM %h\n' | sort -r

shorts.title: List restore points first.
shorts.voiceover: Before you restore anything, list the dated backup manifests. A drill needs a known recovery point, not a guess.
shorts.caption: Start DR with restore point inventory.

linkedin.question: Do your restore drills start with a real list of recovery points?
linkedin.cta: Inventory the backups before choosing one.

youtube.description: List dated backup restore points from manifest files before a restore drill.

experiments.variant_b: A backup you cannot find is not a recovery plan.

title: Read the Backup Manifest
hook: The manifest should say what backup you are about to trust.
problem: You need to confirm the backup id, timestamp, archive name, and completion status before drilling a restore.
starting_state: A backup manifest describes the selected restore point.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command prints the manifest.
when_to_use: Use before extracting an archive so the selected restore point is explicit.
when_not_to_use: Do not rely on a manifest alone; validate the archive and restored files afterward.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Manifest keys including backup_id, created_at, archive, status, and total_files.

shorts.title: Read the manifest.
shorts.voiceover: Before extracting a backup, read the manifest. The status and timestamp are your first sanity check.
shorts.caption: Manifest first, restore second.

linkedin.question: What fields do you require in a backup manifest?
linkedin.cta: Make the restore point explicit before testing it.

youtube.description: Read a backup manifest before restore drill validation.

experiments.variant_b: Do not restore a mystery archive.

title: List Archive Contents Before Extracting
hook: You can inspect a tar backup before it writes a single file.
problem: You need to see what an archive contains before extracting it into a restore sandbox.
command: cd restore-dr && tar -tf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar | sed 's#^./##' | sort
starting_state: A dated backup archive exists under restore-dr/backups.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The tar table of contents is read from the archive.
when_to_use: Use before restore drills, migrations, or incident restores to confirm the archive shape.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat a file listing as byte-level integrity validation.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Sorted archive paths such as app/config.yml, public/index.html, and uploads/avatar.txt.

commands: cd restore-dr && tar -tf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar | sed 's#^./##' | sort | sed -n '1,12p'

shorts.title: List before extract.
shorts.voiceover: Tar can show the archive contents before you extract anything. Use that before a restore drill touches a sandbox.
shorts.caption: Inspect backup contents read-only.

linkedin.question: Do you list archive contents before restore tests?
linkedin.cta: Inspect the tarball before extracting it.

youtube.description: Use tar -tf to inspect a backup archive before extracting it.

experiments.variant_b: Know what the tarball will write.

title: Find Missing Files in an Old Backup
hook: The fastest failed restore drill is the one that finds missing critical files early.
problem: You need to compare a backup archive against a required file list and print required files that are absent.
command: cd restore-dr && tar -tf backups/2026-06-24/site.tar | sed 's#^./##' | sort | comm -23 required-files.txt -
starting_state: The fixture has a required-files.txt list and an older incomplete backup archive.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command compares sorted file lists.
when_to_use: Use during restore drills to catch incomplete backups before relying on them.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it as checksum validation; it only checks required path presence.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Required paths missing from the older backup, such as app/orders.csv and uploads/avatar.txt.

commands: cd restore-dr && tar -tf backups/2026-06-24/site.tar | sed 's#^./##' | sort | comm -23 required-files.txt -

shorts.title: Find missing backup files.
shorts.voiceover: Compare required files against the archive list. Missing paths are restore failures waiting to happen.
shorts.caption: Presence check before trust.

linkedin.question: Which files are mandatory in your restore checklist?
linkedin.cta: Compare backups against a required file list.

youtube.description: Compare a backup archive against required files to find missing restore-critical paths.

experiments.variant_b: Compare the backup to a required list.

title: Extract a Backup Into a Restore Sandbox
hook: A restore drill should write to a sandbox, not production.
problem: You need to extract a backup into a dedicated restore-sandbox directory and list the restored files.
command: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full
starting_state: A restore-sandbox directory exists inside the deterministic fixture.
what_changed: The archive is extracted only under restore-dr/restore-sandbox/full.
when_to_use: Use for restore drills where writing into an isolated sandbox is intentional.
when_not_to_use: Do not run extraction into /, /var/www, or a live application directory during a drill.
undo: Remove the restore-dr/restore-sandbox/full directory from the fixture copy.
expected_output: A restored file list under restore-sandbox/full.

commands: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full
cd restore-dr && find restore-sandbox/full -type f | sed 's#^restore-sandbox/full/##' | sort

shorts.title: Restore into a sandbox.
shorts.voiceover: For drills, extract the archive into a sandbox path. Then validate the restored tree before touching production.
shorts.caption: Sandbox restores reduce risk.

linkedin.question: Do your restore drills ever write outside a sandbox?
linkedin.cta: Keep restore tests isolated and reviewable.

youtube.description: Extract a backup archive into a restore sandbox and list restored files.

experiments.variant_a: Restore drills belong in a sandbox.
experiments.variant_b: Never test restores into production first.

title: Verify Restored File Checksums
hook: A restore is not validated until the bytes match.
problem: You need to run the checksum manifest inside the restored sandbox and confirm each restored file is OK.
command: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full && (cd restore-sandbox/full && sha256sum -c CHECKSUMS.sha256)
starting_state: The archive includes CHECKSUMS.sha256 for critical files.
what_changed: The archive is extracted under restore-sandbox/full, then checksum validation reads restored files.
when_to_use: Use during restore drills when byte-level validation matters.
when_not_to_use: Do not validate against a checksum file from an untrusted or unrelated backup.
undo: Remove the restore-dr/restore-sandbox/full directory from the fixture copy.
expected_output: One OK line per restored file in the checksum manifest.

commands: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full

shorts.title: Verify restored bytes.
shorts.voiceover: File names are not enough. Run the checksum manifest inside the restored sandbox and look for OK on every critical file.
shorts.caption: Restore validation needs checksums.

linkedin.question: Do your restore drills verify bytes or only file names?

youtube.description: Verify restored backup files with sha256sum -c inside a restore sandbox.

experiments.variant_b: Make every restored byte prove itself.

title: Diff Restored Config Against Expected
hook: A restored config can exist and still be the wrong config.
problem: You need to compare a restored configuration file with the expected fixture copy.
command: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full && diff -u expected/app/config.yml restore-sandbox/full/app/config.yml
starting_state: The fixture includes an expected copy of app/config.yml and a backup archive.
what_changed: The archive is extracted under restore-sandbox/full; diff only reads files.
when_to_use: Use when restored configuration must match a known-good expected file.
when_not_to_use: Do not diff secrets into public logs; choose non-sensitive config or redact first.
undo: Remove the restore-dr/restore-sandbox/full directory from the fixture copy.
expected_output: No diff output when the restored config matches expected.

commands: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full

shorts.title: Diff restored config.
shorts.voiceover: After extraction, compare critical config against the expected copy. No diff is the boring result you want.
shorts.caption: Restored config should match expected.

linkedin.question: Which restored config files do you diff during DR drills?
linkedin.cta: Validate critical config, not just file presence.

youtube.description: Use diff -u to compare restored configuration against an expected copy.

experiments.variant_b: Diff restored config before trusting it.

title: Check Required Files After Restore
hook: A successful extraction still needs a required-file check.
problem: You need to confirm every required path exists in the restored sandbox.
command: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full && find restore-sandbox/full -type f | sed 's#^restore-sandbox/full/##' | sort | comm -23 required-files.txt -
starting_state: A required-files.txt checklist and a restore sandbox are available.
what_changed: The archive is extracted under restore-sandbox/full, then file paths are compared.
when_to_use: Use after sandbox extraction to prove required files are present.
when_not_to_use: Do not rely on this alone for content integrity; pair it with checksums.
undo: Remove the restore-dr/restore-sandbox/full directory from the fixture copy.
expected_output: No output means no required files are missing.

commands: cd restore-dr && rm -rf restore-sandbox/full && mkdir -p restore-sandbox/full && tar -xf backups/2026-06-25/site.tar -C restore-sandbox/full && find restore-sandbox/full -type f | sed 's#^restore-sandbox/full/##' | sort | comm -23 required-files.txt -

shorts.title: Check required restored files.
shorts.voiceover: After restore, compare the restored tree to the required list. Silence means every required path is present.
shorts.caption: Required-file checks catch partial restores.

linkedin.question: Do you maintain a required-file list for restore drills?
linkedin.cta: Use a checklist the shell can verify.

youtube.description: Compare restored files against a required-file checklist after sandbox extraction.

experiments.variant_a: A restored tree needs a checklist.
experiments.variant_b: Silence can be the pass signal.

title: Review Critical File Modes in the Archive
hook: Permissions are part of the restore, not decoration.
problem: You need to inspect critical file modes inside the backup archive before trusting a restore.
starting_state: The backup archive contains a secrets file and an executable deploy script.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads archive metadata.
when_to_use: Use during DR validation to confirm important modes are preserved.
when_not_to_use: Do not print sensitive file names from real systems into public logs without review.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Mode strings for app/secrets.env and bin/deploy.sh.

shorts.title: Check backup permissions.
shorts.voiceover: A restore can bring back the right files with the wrong modes. Inspect critical permissions before calling the drill done.
shorts.caption: Permissions are restore data.

linkedin.question: Do your backup checks include permissions?
linkedin.cta: Review critical modes before the incident.

youtube.description: Inspect critical file permissions stored inside a tar backup archive.

experiments.variant_b: The restored file mode can break recovery.

title: Read the Restore Drill Validation Report
hook: A restore drill that leaves no evidence is hard to trust later.
problem: You need to pull the key pass, RPO, RTO, checksum, and file-count lines from a restore drill report.
starting_state: The fixture includes a restore drill validation report under restore-dr/reports.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads the report.
when_to_use: Use after a drill to capture the validation signals people will ask for during an incident review.
when_not_to_use: Do not use a report as a substitute for running the restore and validation commands.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Key report lines showing status, RPO, RTO, checksum, and file-count results.

shorts.title: Keep restore evidence.
shorts.voiceover: End the drill with evidence: status, RPO, RTO, checksum, and file-count results. Future you needs receipts.
shorts.caption: DR validation should leave a report.

linkedin.question: What evidence do you keep from restore drills?
linkedin.cta: Record the pass signals while the drill is fresh.

youtube.description: Extract key validation fields from a restore drill report.

experiments.variant_b: Can you prove the drill passed?
